1: But the foolish plucketh it down with her hands.
2: But he that is he that is perverse in his ways despiseth him.
3: But the lips of the wise shall preserve them.
4: But much increase is is by the strength of the ox.
5: But a false witness will utter lies.
6: But knowledge is is easy unto him that understandeth.
7: When thou perceivest not in him in him the lips of knowledge.
8: But the folly of fools is is deceit.
9: But among the righteous there is there is favour.
10: And a stranger doth not intermeddle with his joy.
11: But the tabernacle of the upright shall flourish.
12: But the end thereof are are the ways of death.
13: And the end of that mirth is is heaviness.
14: And a good man shall be satisfied shall be satisfied from himself.
15: But the prudent man man looketh well to his going.
16: But the fool rageth, and is confident.
17: And a man of wicked devices is hated.
18: But the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
19: And the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
20: But the rich hath hath many friends.
21: But he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is is he.
22: But mercy and truth shall be shall be to them that devise good.
23: But the talk of the lips tendeth tendeth only to penury.
24: But But the foolishness of fools is is folly.
25: But a deceitful witness witness speaketh lies.
26: And his children shall have a place of refuge.
27: To depart from the snares of death.
28: But in the want of people is is the destruction of the prince.
29: But he that is he that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly.
30: But envy the rottenness of the bones.
31: But he that honoureth him hath mercy on the poor.
32: But the righteous hath hope in his death.
33: But that which is that which is in the midst of fools is made known.
34: But sin is is a reproach to any people.
35: But his wrath is against against him that causeth shame.
